title: A simple model on streamflow management with a dynamic risk measure
abstract: We present an exactly-solvable risk-minimizing stochastic differential game for flood management in rivers. The streamflow dynamics follow stochastic differential equations driven by a Levy process. An entropic dynamic risk measure is employed to evaluate a flood risk under model uncertainty. The problem is solved via a Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman-Isaacs equation. We explicitly derive an optimal flood mitigation policy along with its existence criteria and the worst-case probability measure. A backward stochastic differential representation as an alternative formulation is also presented.
